originalTitle: Djibouti's president wins unprecedented sixth term with 97.8% of vote
title: Djibouti's president wins unprecedented sixth term with 97.8% of vote
content: Ismail Omar Guelleh, 78, has extended his already 27-year rule in a poll boycotted by the main opposition candidates.

originalTitle: Nearly 400 sentenced in Nigeria for links to militant Islamists
title: Keeping Everyone Safe in Nigeria!
content:
Hello, my super friends! It's your kid reporter here, bringing you big news from our amazing continent, Africa! Today, we are going to talk about something important that happened in a country called Nigeria. Nigeria is a very big country, full of wonderful people, just like you! Sometimes, even in great places, grown-ups have to make big decisions to keep everyone safe and happy.

Big News from Nigeria!

So, what happened? Well, in Nigeria, there were some people who were making trouble. These people were part of groups that did not follow the rules. They sometimes made other people feel scared or sad. Grown-ups call these groups "militant Islamists." But for us, let's just think of them as people who broke important rules about being kind and peaceful. They did things that were not good for the community. These actions made things difficult and unsafe for others. **Did you know?** Nigeria is the country with the most people in all of Africa! It's home to over 200 million people! That's a lot of friends!

What are Judges and Courts?

When people break rules, especially very big, important rules, grown-ups have special places to figure things out. These places are called "courts." Imagine a special room where grown-ups go to talk about rules and problems to find solutions. In these courts, there are very smart grown-ups called "judges." Judges are like super-teachers for fairness! Their job is to listen very carefully to everyone who speaks. They help make sure that things are fair for everyone and that rules are followed correctly. They want to keep everyone safe and make sure our communities are peaceful and happy.

Making Important Decisions

The judges in Nigeria had a very big and serious job to do. They had to look at what these people from the troubling groups had done. They looked at their actions and how they affected others. They listened to all the stories and looked at all the facts very carefully. After thinking very, very hard, the judges made some important decisions. These decisions are called "sentences." Giving a sentence means deciding what someone should do as a consequence for breaking rules. It's like what happens when you don't share your toys and a grown-up decides you need to sit quietly for a few minutes. A long time ago, people learned that rules help everyone live together nicely. When rules are broken, there are consequences to help fix things. **Did you know?** Many countries in Africa, including Nigeria, use a special system of laws to keep people safe and fair. It's like a big rule book for everyone!

What Happened to the Troublemakers?

The judges decided that nearly 400 people who were part of these groups needed to have consequences. That's almost 400 people! Imagine 400 of your classmates all in one place! It's a very big number of people. These people were given consequences that meant they would spend time away. This is to make sure they cannot cause trouble anymore. Some would spend a few years away, maybe five years. Others would spend a very long time away, sometimes even for their whole lives. This is a very serious consequence for breaking very important rules. It means they won't be able to cause trouble for a long, long time. This helps to protect all the other good and peaceful people in Nigeria. It helps them to feel safe. It's like when you accidentally break a toy, and you might not get to play with it for a while. But for grown-ups, it's about making sure entire communities are safe.

Why Is This Important for Us?

This news might sound a little bit grown-up, but it's important for everyone to understand. It shows us that grown-ups are working hard to make sure our world is safe and peaceful for all of us. When people break rules and hurt others, there are brave judges who step in to make things right and fair. This helps Nigeria to become an even safer place for all the children and families living there. It means more happy playtime, more learning in school, and more peace for everyone. Peace means no fighting or worrying. Every country wants its people to live without fear, and judges help make that happen for communities. **Did you know?** The word "peace" means a time when there is no fighting or trouble, and everyone is calm and happy. We all want peace!

Working Towards a Brighter Future

This big decision by the judges helps Nigeria move forward to a better tomorrow. It shows that bad actions have consequences, and that justice is very important for a happy community. Justice means making things fair and right for everyone. It's like when your friend shares their toys with you, and you share yours back – that's fair! These judges are helping to build a future where everyone can feel safe and loved and live without worry. It also sends a message to other people who might think about breaking rules. It tells them that it's always best to be kind and follow the rules. We all have a part to play in making our world better and safer every day. Even kids can help by being kind to their friends, listening to their teachers, and sharing their toys. Every little bit of kindness helps! **Did you know?** Africa is a continent with many beautiful animals, like elephants and giraffes! They also live by rules in their groups to stay safe and healthy.

What Can We Learn?

We learn that rules are very, very important. They keep us safe at school, at home, and in our countries. We learn that grown-ups like judges work hard to make sure our communities are fair and peaceful for everyone. And we learn that even when there are problems, people are working together to find solutions and make things better. So let's all try to be good friends, follow the rules, and help make our corner of Africa, and the whole world, a happy and safe place! Hooray for peace and safety! Keep shining, super friends!
summary: In Nigeria, judges made big decisions about nearly 400 people who broke important rules, giving them consequences to help keep everyone safe and bring peace.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows that grown-ups like judges work hard to protect people and make sure communities are safe and fair for all children and families in Nigeria.

originalTitle: UK shelves Chagos deal after Trump opposition
title: UK shelves Chagos deal after Trump opposition
content: UK government officials say they are not entirely abandoning the agreement but have run out of time.

originalTitle: Israeli settlers kill Palestinian during raid on occupied West Bank village
title: Israeli settlers kill Palestinian during raid on occupied West Bank village
content: The raid comes just days after Israel approved 34 new illegal settlements in the West Bank.

title: Artemis crew home safely after completing historic mission to the Moon

title: Knifeman calling himself 'Lucifer' slashes three at NYC's Grand Central
content: Police fatally shot the assailant after he ignored at least 20 demands to drop the weapon, said NYPD.

originalTitle: Frontrunner for California governor denies sexual assault allegations
title: Frontrunner for California governor denies sexual assault allegations
content: The Democratic congressman, a leading candidate in the race, has strongly denied the accusations saying he will fight with "facts".

title: Scott nets the winner as Bournemouth stun Arsenal

title: Dewsbury-Hall scores late equaliser as Everton draw at Brentford
content: Kiernan Dewsbury-Hall scores a late equaliser as Everton rescue a point at Brentford after coming from behind twice.

originalTitle: Wieffer scores twice as Brighton beat Burnley
title: Wieffer scores twice as Brighton beat Burnley
